records.ety: [F. blanchet, OF. also blanket, a woolen waistcoat or shirt, the blanket of a printing press; prop. white woolen stuff, dim. of blanc white; blanquette a kind of white pear, fr. blanc white. See Blank, a.]

defs: A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually of wool, and having a nap, used in bed clothing; also, a similar fabric used as a robe; or any fabric used as a cover for a horse.
A piece of rubber, felt, or woolen cloth, used in the tympan to make it soft and elastic.
A streak or layer of blubber in whales.
quotes: Nor heaven peep through the blanket of the dark To cry, “Hold, hold!”
notes: ☞ The use of blankets formerly as curtains in theaters explains the following figure of Shakespeare.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blanketed; p. pr. & vb. n. Blanketing.]

defs: To toss in a blanket by way of punishment.
quotes: We'll have our men blanket 'em i' the hall.

defs: To take the wind out of the sails of (another vessel) by sailing to windward of her.
